findings.title: auditCharacteristics.ts uses 'emits_provenance' but 16a's VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS uses 'provenance'
findings.plan_location: Task 2 Step 3 — AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS table, line 563; Task 2 Step 1 test, line 407
findings.codebase_evidence: docs/composer/ux-redesign-2026-05/16a-phase-7a-backend.md:1190 — VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS contains 'provenance' (not 'emits_provenance'). 16a Round-2 review log (16a line 2323) explicitly states: 'Corrected test fixture emits_provenance → provenance to match 08-catalog-reshape.md authoritative vocabulary'. The 16b plan was not updated to carry this rename.
findings.impact: At staging, every plugin that declares audit_characteristics={'provenance'} (including the canonical CSV example from Task 5 of 16a) will resolve to 'unknown' in lookupAuditCharacteristic and render as a grey fallback chip instead of the green 'extra provenance' icon. The VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS membership test in 16a (test_all_plugin_audit_characteristics_are_valid) would reject 'emits_provenance' if a plugin ever declared it — that's a secondary validation signal.
findings.fix: In auditCharacteristics.ts (plan line 563): change flag: "emits_provenance" to flag: "emits_provenance" → flag: "provenance". In the Task 2 test (plan line 407): change lookupAuditCharacteristic("emits_provenance") → lookupAuditCharacteristic("provenance"). The user-facing label can remain 'extra provenance'.

findings.title: CatalogDrawer.tsx passes onCloseDrawer prop being removed from PluginCard; not listed in Task 5 Modified files
findings.plan_location: Task 5 — File structure (plan lines 885-887); plan File Structure section Modified list (lines 129-138)
findings.codebase_evidence: src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/components/catalog/CatalogDrawer.tsx:359 — `onCloseDrawer={onClose}`. This is the only external caller. The prop `onCloseDrawer?: () => void` is defined in the current PluginCard.tsx:26 and the plan's Task 5 rewrite removes it entirely. The plan does not list CatalogDrawer.tsx as a modified file in either the file-structure header or the Task 5 steps.
findings.impact: After Task 5 commits the new PluginCard.tsx without the onCloseDrawer prop, `npx tsc --noEmit` will fail: TypeScript will report that onCloseDrawer is not a valid prop on PluginCard. The build gate mentioned at Task 5 Step 4 ('npx vitest run src/components/catalog/ src/components/chat/') will also fail if tsc is part of CI. CLAUDE.md explicitly requires: 'Change all call sites in the same commit.'
findings.fix: Add CatalogDrawer.tsx to the Task 5 modified-files list. In that file, remove the `onCloseDrawer={onClose}` prop from the PluginCard invocation at line 359. Add the staged file to the Task 5 git commit command (plan line 1279-1280).

findings.title: AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS table is missing entries for 'retention' and 'network' that are in 16a's VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS
findings.plan_location: Task 2 Step 3 — AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS table (plan lines 482-604); Task 2 Step 1 test (plan line 406)
findings.codebase_evidence: docs/composer/ux-redesign-2026-05/16a-phase-7a-backend.md:1192 ('retention') and :1195 ('network'). Both are in the 13-member VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS frozenset. The 16b AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS table has 11 flags: after fixing F1 (emits_provenance → provenance), it has deterministic, seeded, io_read, io_write, external_call, non_deterministic, quarantine, coerce, provenance, signed, credentials — missing retention and network.
findings.impact: Any plugin that declares audit_characteristics={'retention'} or audit_characteristics={'network'} will render with the grey 'unknown' fallback chip. The 08-catalog-reshape.md mockup (line 73) shows '✓ retention' in the CSV example card — if CSVSource declares 'retention' in 16a Task 5, it will render as unknown. Additionally, the Task 2 Step 1 test description at plan line 406 says 'exposes provenance / retention / quarantine / coerce / signed' but the test body (lines 407-410) only asserts lookupAuditCharacteristic for emits_provenance (sic provenance), quarantine, coerce, signed — retention is named in the test description but NOT tested.
findings.fix: Add metadata entries for 'retention' and 'network' to the AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS array in auditCharacteristics.ts. Suggested entries: retention (label: 'retention policy', glyph: '📋', tone: 'positive', tooltip: references retention/purge lifecycle); network (label: 'network', glyph: '🌐', tone: 'attention', tooltip: similar to external_call — note that 'network' as a declared characteristic is distinct from 'external_call' which is Determinism-inferred). Also add a test assertion `expect(lookupAuditCharacteristic('retention')).not.toBeNull()` in the Task 2 test to match the test description.

findings.title: Task 1 test imports only { describe, it, expectTypeOf } but uses expect() on line 220
findings.plan_location: Task 1 Step 1 test, plan line 184 (import) and line 220 (usage)
findings.codebase_evidence: vite.config.ts:14 — `globals: true`. With vitest globals enabled, expect() is injected globally, so the missing import does not cause a runtime failure.
findings.impact: The test will pass without the import. However it is inconsistent with the rest of the test files in this codebase (all other test files explicitly import expect) and creates a misleading pattern for future editors. If globals are ever disabled, this test will silently break.
findings.fix: Change plan line 184 to: `import { describe, it, expect, expectTypeOf } from "vitest";`

findings.title: New PluginCard.tsx comment says 'Re-exported here' for PREFILL_CHAT_INPUT_EVENT
findings.plan_location: Task 5 Step 3 proposed PluginCard.tsx, plan lines 1057-1060
findings.codebase_evidence: The comment block reads 'Re-exported here for backwards compatibility'. Per the 2026-05-15 review history (plan line 1359), this was already corrected from 're-export' to 'existing direct export'. The proposed component code block still has the 're-exported' wording in the JSDoc comment (line 1058: 'Re-exported here for backwards compatibility').
findings.impact: Misleading comment — it is not a re-export; it is the authoritative export location.
findings.fix: Change plan line 1058 to: `/** Event name dispatched by InlineChatSourceEntry and consumed by ChatInput.tsx. Defined here because PluginCard.tsx is its historical home; ChatInput.tsx imports it from this path. Do not rename or move. */`

findings.title: Commit message says 'existing direct export at PluginCard.tsx:17' but the rewrite shifts that line
findings.plan_location: Task 5 Step 6 commit message, plan lines 1292-1293
findings.codebase_evidence: In the CURRENT file, PREFILL_CHAT_INPUT_EVENT is at line 17 (verified). In the PROPOSED rewrite, the 13-line header comment block plus four import lines push the declaration to approximately line 22. The claim ':17' refers to the pre-rewrite file, which is accurate as a 'was' citation but may mislead a future reader looking at the post-commit file.
findings.impact: Low — commit message is documentation, not a test assertion. Does not affect execution.
findings.fix: Remove the line-number citation from the commit message, or say 'PluginCard.tsx (see top-of-file)' instead.

findings.title: Task 5 wholesale-replaces PluginCard.test.tsx, dropping all discriminated-union coverage
findings.codebase_evidence: Current PluginCard.test.tsx:115-292 covers isDiscriminated, resolveVariants, dangling $ref, missing discriminator mapping, empty-variant cases. All these code paths survive in the proposed PluginCard rewrite (plan lines 1088-1113: isDiscriminated, resolveVariants are retained verbatim). The new 10-test suite in Task 5 Step 1 contains no tests for these paths.
findings.impact: Production code paths retained, test coverage removed. This is a reality-lens observation (the code and the test gap are both real) rather than a hallucination finding. The reviewer lens does not assess coverage sufficiency, but the fact that the production paths are intentionally kept while their tests are dropped is noted.
findings.fix: Add discriminated-union rendering tests back to the new PluginCard.test.tsx (adapting the existing cases to the new test fixture factory which requires the Phase-7A fields). The tests at PluginCard.test.tsx:95-292 are directly portable with the makePlugin() factory extension.

blocking_issues: F1: auditCharacteristics.ts flag 'emits_provenance' must be renamed to 'provenance' to match 16a's VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS (16a:1190) and the 16a Round-2 vocabulary correction. Fix requires changing plan line 563 (flag value) and plan line 407 (test lookup).

F2: CatalogDrawer.tsx:359 passes onCloseDrawer={onClose} to PluginCard. Task 5 removes the prop but does not list CatalogDrawer.tsx in Modified files. Add CatalogDrawer.tsx to Task 5 Modified list and remove the onCloseDrawer prop pass-through in the same commit.
warnings: F3 (MAJOR): AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS table missing 'retention' and 'network' entries that exist in 16a's VALID_AUDIT_CHARACTERISTICS. Plugins declaring these flags will render as unknown chips. The Task 2 test description names 'retention' but never asserts it.

F7 (NIT/coverage): Task 5 replaces PluginCard.test.tsx wholesale and the new suite drops all discriminated-union tests (isDiscriminated, resolveVariants, dangling-$ref). Those code paths are retained in the rewrite. Not a reality-lens finding but noted for completeness.
